title: To A Lady With A Withered Violet.
content:
Though fate upon this faded flower
    His withering hand has laid,
    Its odour'd breath defies his power,
    Its sweets are undecayed.

    And thus, although thy warbled strains
    No longer wildly thrill,
    The memory of the song remains,
    Its soul is with me still.
meaning:
Joseph Rodman Drake's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "To A Lady With A Withered Violet."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
